extra.reasoning: The property globally(opted_out implies not sent) holds. The invariant (opted_out implies not sent) is inductive over all four transitions: (1) t_optout sets opted_out:=true AND sent:=false simultaneously, so it cannot create a state with opted_out=true and sent=true. (2) t_send_allowed is the ONLY transition that sets sent:=true, but its guard requires not opted_out and it leaves opted_out unchanged at false, so the resulting state has opted_out=false (invariant holds vacuously). (3) t_send_blocked fires only when opted_out=true and sets sent:=false, preserving the invariant. (4) t_tick sets sent:=false and leaves opted_out unchanged, so sent is false afterward regardless. The initial state (opted_out=false, sent=false) satisfies the invariant. Since the only way to make sent true requires opted_out to be false, and the only way to make opted_out true simultaneously forces sent false, the conjunction (opted_out=true and sent=true) is never reachable on any execution (including all executions up to 8 events).
